ELPAC Level 4
Lexile 650–1000L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Grandmother's Line, Yasmin's Curve

STS-MS8-L4-6501000-MSMATH8-PCI-MNA-01 • MS Math Grade 8 • Linear and Non-Linear Functions
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Yasmin's podcast, Numbers and Stories, interviews her grandmother's bakery about how it grew. Bread sales climb the same amount every day, so that graph is a straight line. The podcast's listeners double and double again, so that graph is a curve that shoots up. One graph is linear and one is not.
Which graph would you rather be: the steady straight line, or the curve that keeps bending up faster?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Yasmin doing?
Look at the picture. Say what Yasmin is doing and give one reason from the picture that proves it.
2
What is Karim holding?
Look at the picture. Say what Karim is holding and give one clue that proves it.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say what the teacher is doing and give one clue that proves it.
4
Where are they? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say what place this is and give two things you see that prove it.
5
Tell me about the graph board.
Look at the picture. Say how the two graphs differ and explain what makes one linear and one not.
6
What is Layla wearing?
Look at the picture. Say what Layla is wearing and explain how it helps you pick her out.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS 8.F.A.3 — Linear and Non-Linear Functions. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
